rows.title: Local editor, command, Git, and configuration core
rows.summary: Editing, command surfaces, local Git, and layered configuration remain available with no account, no managed control plane, and no seat assignment. Org-switch and seat-quota state do not gate this row.
rows.local_core_continuity: Local editor, command, Git, and configuration core work without managed identity, network reachability, seat assignment, or quota budget.

org_switch_behavior.summary: Switching organizations leaves open buffers, local Git state, and on-disk profile files untouched. Managed projections rebind on the next managed action.

grace_window.summary: Local core does not consume a managed grace window.

seat_quota.summary: Local core is not seat- or quota-gated.

offboarding.summary: Offboarding from a managed org does not remove local files, local Git state, or already-generated support bundles.

rows.notes: The local-only floor that every other row narrows against. Beta managed/paid surfaces MUST NOT widen so they erode this row.

rows.title: Extension registry and signed mirror
rows.summary: Registry browsing and install routes reach the public registry, a customer-operated mirror, or an air-gapped signed bundle. Already-installed extensions continue to execute when the registry is unreachable.
rows.local_core_continuity: Already-installed extensions execute regardless of registry or marketplace reachability; local discovery via CLI or local bundle remains available.

org_switch_behavior.summary: Org switch rebinds the active mirror and any signed policy bundle to the new org; cached registry metadata is invalidated and re-resolved.

grace_window.summary: When the live registry is unreachable, cached metadata stays usable for browsing/install for a short window before clients fall back to mirror or file import only.

seat_quota.summary: Registry downloads are metered as service metadata; no per-seat gate is applied for browsing or install.

offboarding.summary: On managed-access end, hosted marketplace access stops; local, mirror, and file-import install routes remain.

rows.absence_narrows_to: With no live registry, install narrows to mirror, file import, and CLI-attached local bundles; discovery falls back to the registry strawman index until the next mirror sync.
rows.notes: Mirror posture is the air-gap floor for beta extension publication.

rows.title: Identity envelope and signed policy distribution
rows.summary: Standards-based identity (OIDC) and signed policy bundles can be customer-operated, mirrored, or vendor-managed. Local core continues under last-known policy when the policy service is unreachable.
rows.local_core_continuity: Local editing, search, Git, tasks, and local AI continue under local configuration or last-known policy when the policy service is unreachable.

org_switch_behavior.summary: Switching organizations requires the new org's signed identity envelope and policy bundle. Managed actions deny until the handoff completes; local core is unaffected.

grace_window.summary: Last-known signed policy stays in effect during the grace window; managed actions degrade to restricted-managed-only until the policy bundle refreshes or the window expires.

seat_quota.summary: The identity envelope itself is not seat-metered; downstream managed surfaces may still be.

offboarding.summary: Offboarding snapshots the entitlement state, freezes managed writes, exposes export windows, and issues a destruction receipt when managed access ends. Local files remain.

rows.absence_narrows_to: Without a live policy service, managed actions narrow to restricted-managed-only until a fresh signed bundle is imported or the grace window expires.
rows.notes: The self-hostable floor under every managed surface that needs org identity.

rows.title: Remote workspace control plane
rows.summary: Managed workspace orchestration is a hosted convenience layered on a self-hostable protocol. Direct local workspaces and direct SSH or container attach remain available outside the control plane.
rows.local_core_continuity: Local folders, direct SSH or container attach, and local Git remain user-controlled. Workspace orchestration loss does not block local editing.

org_switch_behavior.summary: Org switch rebinds the workspace fleet to the new org's control plane. Live sessions in the prior org freeze and export rather than transfer.

grace_window.summary: On entitlement loss, running workspaces enter a short export window before managed access ends.

seat_quota.summary: Workspace hours are metered. Quota exhaustion pauses new managed sessions; running sessions degrade to export-only.

offboarding.summary: Offboarding announces the cutoff, freezes managed writes, exposes a usage export window, ends managed access, and issues a destruction receipt.

rows.absence_narrows_to: Without a managed control plane, work narrows to local folders and direct SSH or container attach. Managed workspace hour meters do not transfer to local work.
rows.notes: Managed workspace beta gates seat and quota state on managed sessions only; local core continues.

rows.title: AI provider routing and policy gateway
rows.summary: Managed AI gateway provides quota, redaction, and audit on top of provider routing. Local and BYOK AI routes remain distinct.
rows.local_core_continuity: Local and BYOK AI routes continue independent of the managed gateway. Loss of managed AI does not disable edit, search, Git, or tasks.

org_switch_behavior.summary: Org switch rebinds gateway routing, redaction, and audit to the new org's policy. In-flight requests complete under the prior org's policy and are not silently re-attributed.

grace_window.summary: On entitlement loss, audit and export remain accessible for a short window; new gateway-routed requests deny.

seat_quota.summary: Gateway tokens and tool invocations are metered. Quota exhaustion denies new managed requests; local and BYOK routes remain available.

offboarding.summary: Managed AI offboarding exposes the usage export and audit snapshot; local and BYOK paths persist after managed access ends.

rows.absence_narrows_to: With no managed gateway, AI narrows to BYOK and local model routes. Managed redaction and audit no longer apply.
rows.notes: Gateway quota narrows the managed request only; the local AI contract is separate.

rows.title: Managed AI quota, audit, and billing surface
rows.summary: Hosted UI for managed AI quota, audit, and seat-bound billing. Denial pauses managed AI for the affected seat; local and BYOK AI routes are unaffected.
rows.local_core_continuity: Loss of paid AI seat narrows managed AI only. Edit, search, Git, tasks, and BYOK AI remain available.

org_switch_behavior.summary: Switching to an organization with no paid AI seat denies managed AI until a seat is assigned. The prior org's audit export remains downloadable for the grace window.

grace_window.summary: On seat revocation, audit and export are available for a short window before managed-access end. New managed AI requests deny immediately.

seat_quota.summary: Paid AI is gated by an assigned seat and a per-seat quota. Seat revocation denies managed AI; quota exhaustion denies new managed requests but preserves audit/export.

offboarding.summary: Offboarding announces the cutoff, freezes new managed AI writes, exposes the usage export window, ends managed access, and issues a destruction receipt for usage records.

rows.absence_narrows_to: With no paid seat, managed AI narrows to denied. BYOK and local AI routes remain available.
rows.notes: The canonical paid/seat-bound beta row; every later paid seat surface inherits its org-switch, grace-window, and offboarding semantics from this row unless it declares its own.

rows.title: Managed settings sync
rows.summary: Hosted profile and settings sync on top of customer-managed storage or local profile files. Sync is optional; local files remain authoritative.
rows.local_core_continuity: Local profile files remain the source of truth. Sync disable or seat revocation does not mutate or remove local files.

org_switch_behavior.summary: Org switch rebinds sync to the new org's storage and policy. Profiles from the prior org are exportable but not silently merged into the new org.

grace_window.summary: On seat loss, last-synced profile remains usable locally. Managed writes are frozen until the seat is reinstated or the export window expires.

seat_quota.summary: Sync is gated by a seat and a per-seat byte quota; quota exhaustion freezes managed sync writes only.

offboarding.summary: Offboarding exposes the profile export, ends managed sync, and issues a destruction receipt; local profile files remain on disk.

rows.absence_narrows_to: With no paid seat, managed sync narrows to local profile files only. Export and file-import remain available.
rows.notes: Paid sync rows MUST preserve local profile files at every seat state.

rows.title: Support ingest and telemetry pipeline
rows.summary: Hosted or customer-operated support ingest carries opt-in support uploads. Local and offline support bundles remain the canonical fallback.
rows.local_core_continuity: Local and offline support bundles can always be generated and inspected without upload.

org_switch_behavior.summary: Org switch rebinds support ingest routing. Already-uploaded bundles stay with the prior org's retention window and are not silently re-attributed.

grace_window.summary: On entitlement loss, existing bundles remain downloadable for a short window; new uploads deny.

seat_quota.summary: Support ingest is metered by bundle count, not by seat; quota exhaustion pauses new uploads.

offboarding.summary: Offboarding announces the cutoff, exposes existing bundles for download, then ends managed access. Local bundle generation persists.

rows.absence_narrows_to: With no managed ingest, support narrows to local bundle generation and manual transfer.
rows.notes: Managed support ingest is an upload convenience; local export remains the supportability floor.

rows.title: Fleet admin UI and SCIM dashboards
rows.summary: Hosted admin UI for fleet identity, policy, and entitlement review. Self-hosted identity and signed policy bundles remain the standards-based fallback.
rows.local_core_continuity: Removing admin UI access does not corrupt local clones, unsaved buffers, or already-exported policy bundles.

org_switch_behavior.summary: Admin handoff is required to bind the dashboard to a new org. Without admin handoff, the dashboard denies until resolved; self-hosted identity continues.

grace_window.summary: Audit and entitlement snapshot remain accessible for a short window after admin access ends; mutation denies.

offboarding.summary: Offboarding exposes the entitlement snapshot export window, ends admin access, and issues a destruction receipt.

rows.absence_narrows_to: Without admin UI access, fleet posture narrows to file-based signed bundles and standards-based identity.
rows.notes: Admin actions are admin-identity-bound; user surfaces narrow independently.

rows.title: Managed cloud daily-driver parity (reserved)
rows.summary: Reserved out-of-scope row preventing managed-cloud daily driver from being implied by other beta managed rows.
rows.local_core_continuity: Local-first beta scope continues; managed-cloud daily-driver parity is not claimed.

grace_window.summary: No managed-cloud daily-driver grace window is claimed for beta.

rows.notes: This reserved row exists so managed beta rows do not silently imply hosted workspace parity at beta.
